# SC-13: DAST

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Dynamic Application Security Testing: execute runtime security tests against a deployed test instance including injection attack tests, authentication flaw detection, session management validation, TLS configuration checks, and other OWASP top-10 categories.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 4 | Testing & Documentation | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Execute runtime security test suite against test environment; generate findings report. |
| **Human does** | Review findings; remediate or formally accept residual risk per severity policy. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| DAST scan report | evidence | Runtime security test results with findings, severity ratings, and remediation recommendations. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_release |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | DAST tool (e.g. OWASP ZAP, Burp Suite) |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

Critical and high-severity findings block Stage 4 exit.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 24 | Requirements for ICT testing of security controls |
| DORA | Art. 25 | Testing of ICT tools and systems including penetration testing |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(3) | Technical solutions for AI-specific cybersecurity including runtime attack simulation |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Security Architect
